description_long: This is a garden or leaf rake with a long, straight wooden handle and a wide, fan-shaped head made of green metal. The head features numerous evenly spaced, thin metal tines that curve outward and downward, ideal for gathering leaves or debris. The handle is cylindrical and smooth, with a natural wood finish, and connects securely to the metal head. The green metal head is slightly domed at the center, providing structural strength and a broad sweeping area. The tines are uniformly arranged in a radial pattern, giving the rake a symmetrical and efficient design. There are no visible brand markings, grip enhancements, or signs of wear on the object.
description: A long-handled rake with a green, fan-shaped metal head and evenly spaced curved tines, attached to a smooth wooden handle. The design is clean, symmetrical, and appears unused.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the green fan-shaped head and the radial arrangement of the tines.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the rake, the curvature of the tines, and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the green head and the even spread of the tines from the handle connection.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the profile of the rake and the outward curve of the tines.
attribute_handle_type: The handle is long, straight, cylindrical, and made of smooth, natural-finish wood with a round cross-section and no visible grip enhancements or end caps.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: The tines are thin, metallic, evenly spaced, and radiate outward in a fan shape from the head, all of uniform length and slightly curved downward.
attribute_mounting_position: Designed to be leaned vertically against a wall or hung by the handle; no dedicated mounting feature is visible.
